intro: The Department of Youth and Community Development was formed in 1996 through the merger of the Department of Youth Services and the Community Development Agency. DYCD invests in a citywide network of community-based organizations to alleviate the effects of poverty, funding programs such as afterschool care, Beacon and Cornerstone community centers, runaway and homeless youth services, and youth workforce development.

cardHighlights.function: Funds youth programs, afterschool care, and workforce development
cardHighlights.scale: Placed a record 97,444 young people in summer jobs in 2025
